senses.definition: to apply a new coating or material to reconstruct the surface of something, especially a road or pavement

examples.x: The city decided to resurface the main roads to improve the overall driving experience.
To address wear and tear, the homeowners opted to resurface their driveway with fresh asphalt.
The construction crew worked overnight to resurface the worn-out parking lot with a new layer of concrete.
In preparation for the upcoming event, the municipality chose to resurface the public square.
The highway maintenance team regularly resurfaces roads to ensure safety and efficiency.

senses.definition: the construction or maintenance activities carried out on roads, highways, or streets
an area on a road where workers are building or repairing something, such as bridges or highways
a thick, black, sticky substance used in road construction and maintenance for paving and sealing surfaces

examples.x: Workers spread hot tar to fill the cracks in the road.
She smelled the strong odor of tar as they resurfaced the street.
He accidentally stepped in a fresh tar patch.
They used tar to waterproof the new bridge deck.
The tar hardened quickly in the summer heat.

senses.definition: a type of road surface made of tar mixed with crushed stone, commonly used for runways and road

examples.x: Workers laid fresh tarmac on the main highway.
She preferred driving on tarmac roads for their smoothness.
The airplane taxied smoothly on the tarmac.
They sealed the tarmac to extend its lifespan.
The tarmac needed resurfacing after years of heavy use.

senses.definition: a black sticky substance which is the combination of dark bituminous pitch with sand or gravel, commonly used in building roads
a black, sticky mixture used for paving roads and pathways

examples.x: The crew spread blacktop over the gravel base.
She drove carefully on the newly laid blacktop.
They used blacktop to pave the new parking lot.
He watched the steamroller smooth out the blacktop.
The blacktop provided a durable surface for the playground.

senses.definition: the small, rounded stones used historically for paving streets and paths

examples.x: The old town square was paved with cobblestones.
She loved the charm of the cobblestone streets.
They replaced the damaged cobblestones with new ones.
He tripped on the uneven cobblestones.
The cobblestones were slippery when wet.

senses.definition: a mix of sand and small pebbles that cover the surface of some roads
a type of road construction that uses layers of compacted crushed stone, bound with tar or asphalt

examples.x: The rural road was built using the macadam method.
She appreciated the durability of the macadam surface.
They repaired the macadam road after the heavy rain.
He studied the history of macadam road construction.
The macadam pavement provided a stable driving surface.

senses.definition: a hard material used for building structures, made by mixing cement, water, sand, and small stones

examples.x: The workers poured concrete to create a sturdy foundation for the house.
The sidewalk was made of concrete, providing a durable surface for pedestrians.
She designed a beautiful patio using colored concrete for an attractive finish.
They added rebar to the concrete for extra reinforcement.
The construction project involved a large amount of concrete for various structures.

senses.definition: a small, often deep, depression in a road surface caused by wear, weather, and traffic

examples.x: He swerved to avoid a large pothole in the street.
The pothole caused damage to his car's suspension.
They filled the pothole with asphalt to prevent further damage.
She reported the pothole to the city's maintenance department.
Potholes often form after the winter freeze-thaw cycles.

senses.definition: a groove or track made in a road by cars driving over it many times

examples.x: The dirt road was full of deep ruts after the heavy rain.
She navigated carefully to avoid the ruts in the gravel path.
Ruts can develop quickly on unpaved roads.
They graded the road to smooth out the ruts.
The ruts made driving difficult for small cars.

senses.definition: a versatile heavy equipment machine with a digging bucket on the rear and a loader bucket on the front, commonly used in construction and utility work

examples.x: The construction crew used a backhoe to dig a trench for the new water pipes.
The farmer rented a backhoe to remove tree stumps from his field.
Workers relied on the backhoe to load gravel into the truck efficiently.
The landscapers used a backhoe to reshape the ground for the garden project.

senses.definition: a heavy vehicle with a large, smooth roller used for flattening and compressing surfaces during road construction

examples.x: He watched the steamroller at work on the new road.
The steamroller pressed the asphalt into a smooth surface.
She heard the rumble of the steamroller from her house.
The steamroller operator ensured the road was level.
They rented a steamroller for the paving project.

senses.definition: a type of heavy machinery used in construction and agriculture for lifting and transporting materials
a large, powerful vehicle with a wide steel blade at its front that is used to destroy buildings or move earth
a vehicle used in construction to lay asphalt or concrete on roads and pavements
a heavy-duty vehicle with a bed that can be tilted or raised at the front, allowing it to unload its contents by tipping them out behind the truck

examples.x: After filling the dump truck with sand, they drove it to the building site.
The workers used a dump truck to haul away the piles of dirt from the construction site.
The construction crew needed a larger dump truck to carry away the debris from the demolition.
They rented a dump truck to move all the landscaping materials for the project.

senses.definition: the process of removing a layer of asphalt or concrete from a road or pavement surface

examples.x: Pavement milling is often used to repair damaged roads by removing the old surface before applying a new one.
The noise and dust generated during pavement milling can inconvenience nearby residents and businesses.
The machines used for pavement milling are equipped with rotating drum cutters that grind away the old pavement.
After pavement milling is completed, the surface is typically inspected for any remaining debris or irregularities.
Pavement milling improves the smoothness and texture of roads, enhancing driving safety and comfort for motorists.

senses.definition: a vehicle equipped with a tank and sprayer used to evenly distribute asphalt onto roads during construction or maintenance
a heavy vehicle used in construction to level and smooth surfaces such as roads and foundations
a vehicle or other piece of equipment used for clearing roads of snow

examples.x: The snowplow cleared the streets early in the morning, making it safe for cars to drive.
After a heavy snowfall, the city deployed multiple snowplows to ensure all main roads were passable.
He attached a snowplow to the front of his truck to clear his long driveway more efficiently.
The airport used specialized snowplows to keep the runways free of snow and ice during the winter storm.
Residents appreciated the prompt response of the snowplow drivers who worked tirelessly through the night.

senses.definition: ‌a large vehicle used for putting salt, sand or grit on the roads in winter when there is ice on them

examples.x: The city sent out a salt truck early in the morning to keep the roads safe after the snowfall.
A salt truck passed by our street last night, so the ice melted quickly.
Drivers had to be careful because the salt truck had not yet treated the highway.
I had to wait for the salt truck to pass before driving down the icy road.

senses.definition: a thin, nearly invisible layer of ice on a road surface, often causing dangerous driving conditions

examples.x: He warned drivers about the black ice on the bridge.
They treated the roads to prevent the formation of black ice.
She skidded on black ice and nearly lost control of her car.
Black ice is especially hazardous in the early morning hours.
The black ice caught many drivers by surprise.

senses.definition: to scatter small, rough particles like sand or salt on a road to make it less slippery and melt ice

examples.x: The workers gritted the roads before the snowstorm.
They used a truck to grit the icy roads.
She appreciated the effort to grit the parking lot.
He gritted the sidewalk to prevent slipping.
The city plans to grit the roads after the freezing rain.

senses.definition: a schematic representation that displays the layout, intersections, and distances along a specific route

examples.x: Engineers use straight-line diagrams to plan the alignment and dimensions of roads, ensuring safe and efficient traffic flow.
Before construction begins, urban planners often create straight-line diagrams to visualize how new roads will integrate into existing transportation networks.
Drivers rely on straight-line diagrams provided by highway authorities to navigate unfamiliar routes and anticipate upcoming exits.
Municipalities update straight-line diagrams regularly to reflect changes such as new intersections or road expansions.
During highway maintenance projects, workers refer to straight-line diagrams to pinpoint areas requiring repair or improvement.

senses.definition: a fictional or nonexistent street created on paper but not physically built

examples.x: The developers promised to build a new neighborhood, but it turned out to be just a paper street on the city's planning maps.
Residents were disappointed when they discovered that their dream home was located on a paper street that didn't actually exist.
The city council debated whether to remove the paper street designation from the zoning plans due to lack of funding for construction.
Some investors were misled into buying property on what turned out to be a paper street, causing legal disputes over ownership.
Surveyors identified several paper streets that were planned decades ago but never developed into actual roads.

senses.definition: a short, dead-end road that is often planned for future extension but currently terminates without connecting to other streets or throughways

examples.x: The stub street near our neighborhood ends abruptly, surrounded by undeveloped land.
The city council discussed extending the stub street to improve access to the industrial area.
The children played basketball on the makeshift court at the end of the stub street.
The delivery truck struggled to turn around in the narrow space of the stub street.
The real estate agent warned us that the property's value might be affected by its location on a stub street with no immediate plans for extension.
